planning_note: Planning/reference file only. This is not wired into app behavior and must be reviewed before any app-consumable bundle files are created.
ranking_note: This is a practical ServSync library priority list, not a guaranteed national job-volume ranking. Public sources generally list common HVAC services and repairs, not verified job-count rankings.

work_category_normalization_note.implementation_requirement: Before creating app-consumable bundle files, each curator job type must be mapped into one approved ServSync work category.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Most repair work starts with a diagnostic visit before a contractor can recommend repair, replacement, or follow-up work.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for service call fee, inspection labor, system findings note, repair options, and exclusions.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Tune-ups and maintenance plans are core recurring HVAC work and useful for homeowner retention.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for filter check, coil cleaning, condensate check, thermostat check, electrical inspection note, and customer maintenance notes.

candidate_job_types.why_include: High-value residential job type and a major estimate workflow for HVAC contractors.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for equipment removal, condenser install, coil/air handler coordination, line set review, thermostat, startup, disposal, permit/admin, and exclusions.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Heat pumps are common residential replacement options, especially where one system handles heating and cooling.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for outdoor unit, air handler, thermostat, auxiliary heat review, duct compatibility, startup, permit/admin, and code-sensitive items.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Common heating replacement job and important for contractors in cooler regions.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for remove existing furnace, set new furnace, gas connection review, venting review, condensate where applicable, thermostat, startup, permit/admin, and exclusions.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Useful for additions, garages, bonus rooms, older homes without ductwork, and room-specific comfort problems.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for indoor head, outdoor unit, line set, wall penetration, condensate routing, electrical coordination, startup, permit/admin, and exclusions.

candidate_job_types.bundle_name: Thermostat Replacement or Smart Thermostat Installation
candidate_job_types.why_include: Common small-ticket job that can be recommended after comfort complaints, system upgrades, or homeowner requests.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for remove existing thermostat, install customer-selected thermostat, setup, compatibility review, wiring/common-wire note, and customer instruction.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Capacitor failure is one of the most common AC repair recommendations and is easy to structure into estimate items.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Good fit for diagnostic confirmation, capacitor replacement, electrical inspection note, startup test, and customer warning about related failures.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Common outdoor-unit repair tied to no-cool calls, overheating, noise, or outdoor fan failure.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Good fit for fan motor, capacitor if needed, blade inspection, electrical connections, startup test, and related-condition notes.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Common airflow-related HVAC repair that applies to furnaces, air handlers, and some heat pump systems.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Good fit for blower motor, wheel inspection, capacitor/module review, access labor, startup test, and airflow notes.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Very common AC service issue and important because drain problems can cause leaks, shutoffs, and homeowner callbacks.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for drain clearing, pan inspection, float switch review, PVC repair, access notes, water damage exclusion, and maintenance recommendation.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Common enough to need structure, but must be handled carefully because refrigerant work is regulated and diagnosis-dependent.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for leak search, repair allowance, recovery/handling note, pressure test, evacuation, recharge, and replacement recommendation when repair is not practical.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Frequent follow-up recommendation after refrigerant leak, frozen coil, or poor cooling diagnosis.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for recover refrigerant, remove coil, install coil, line connection, drain/pan review, evacuation, recharge, startup, and exclusions.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Major repair that often competes with full system replacement, making estimate clarity important.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for compressor replacement, refrigerant recovery, filter drier, evacuation, recharge, electrical testing, startup, and replacement-option note.

candidate_job_types.bundle_name: Furnace Ignition or Flame Sensor Repair
candidate_job_types.why_include: Common no-heat repair category with clear line-item recommendations.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Good fit for diagnostic confirmation, flame sensor cleaning/replacement, igniter replacement, burner inspection note, startup test, and safety review reminder.

candidate_job_types.bundle_name: Furnace Gas Valve or Control Board Repair
candidate_job_types.why_include: Common heating repair path for no-heat calls, but needs review because gas and control work can be safety/code sensitive.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Good fit for diagnostic confirmation, gas valve/control board replacement, wiring check, startup test, combustion/safety check note, and exclusions.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Common comfort and efficiency-related work, especially for leaky, disconnected, damaged, or poorly sealed duct runs.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for accessible duct repair, mastic/foil tape sealing, insulation repair, register boot sealing, attic/crawlspace access notes, and exclusions.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Common add-on or correction item during system replacement, comfort complaints, additions, and airflow issues.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for duct removal, new duct run, transition, return/supply modification, insulation, access notes, balancing note, and concealed-condition exclusions.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Important replacement category for heat pump and split-system homes where the indoor unit is replaced separately or with the outdoor unit.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for remove existing air handler, set new unit, drain/pan, electrical connection review, duct transition, startup, permit/admin, and exclusions.

candidate_job_types.bundle_name: Indoor Air Quality Filter or Air Purifier Installation
candidate_job_types.why_include: Common HVAC upsell and useful recommendation when customers mention dust, allergies, odors, pets, or filter upgrades.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Good fit for media cabinet, filter upgrade, air purifier, electrical coordination where applicable, customer maintenance note, and exclusions.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Especially useful in humid markets and homes with comfort, moisture, or indoor air quality complaints.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Good fit for equipment install, duct connection, condensate routing, control wiring, electrical review, startup, and exclusions.

candidate_job_types.why_include: Useful for homes with hot/cold spots, multi-level comfort issues, additions, or customers wanting room/zone control.
candidate_job_types.estimate_bundle_fit: Strong fit for dampers, zone control board, thermostats/sensors, duct review, bypass/static pressure review, startup, and exclusions.

recommended_defer_until_after_core_libra.reason: Important HVAC work, but outside the first residential/small-contractor library focus.
Important in some regions, but less universal across the first HVAC bundle set.
Useful but more specialized and complex than first-pass residential HVAC service bundles.
Common service, but can be controversial and varies by contractor offering; better after duct repair/sealing and indoor air quality bundles.
